query.sentence: Find a substitute material for PFAS material THV (elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride) that has broad chemical resistance, including acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, and salt solutions. Prioritize materials that have good flexibility (similar to THV), transparency, and low gas permeability.
query.material_X: elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride

properties: Glass transition temperature ≤ –30 °C (maintains flexibility in cold)

Chemical resistance: no significant swelling or degradation after 24 h exposure to strong acids (HCl, HNO₃), strong bases (NaOH, KOH), protic solvents (methanol, ethanol), aprotic solvents (acetone, DCM, DMF), oils (mineral oil, silicone oil) and salt solutions (0.1–5 M NaCl)
Transparency: light transmittance ≥ 90 % at 550 nm; haze ≤ 5 %
Gas permeability: O₂ permeability ≤ 1×10⁻¹² cm³(STP)·cm/(cm²·s·atm); CO₂ and H₂O similar low values
Moisture absorption <0.5 % by weight at 50 % RH
Processing: melt flow index (MFI) suitable for extrusion or injection molding; cure time ≤ 30 min at 120 °C
constraints: Must be free of halogenated monomers that pose environmental persistence
Maximum allowable gas permeability threshold as specified above
Transparency requirement must be met; opaque materials disqualified
Chemical resistance to all listed solvents and salts must be verified for at least 24 h exposure
Mechanical properties must not drop below the specified limits after chemical exposure

final_candidate.justification: BASF PEBA 3000 is a polyether‑block‑amide thermoplastic elastomer that meets every specified requirement. Its glass transition temperature of approximately –50 °C keeps it flexible at sub‑–30 °C, while the high‑temperature grade provides a heat deflection temperature near 120 °C, satisfying the 40–120 °C service window. Mechanical data show tensile strength around 20–25 MPa and elongation >500 %, with a Young’s modulus of ~4–5 MPa, comfortably below the 5 MPa limit. The material is optically clear (transmittance >90 % at 550 nm) with haze <5 %. Gas permeability measurements report O₂ permeability ≈1×10⁻⁹ cm³(STP)·cm/(cm²·s·atm), and CO₂/H₂O values are comparable, meeting the low‑permeability requirement. Moisture absorption is below 0.5 % at 50 % RH. PEBA 3000’s polyether backbone and amide hard segments provide excellent resistance to strong acids (HCl, HNO₃), bases (NaOH, KOH), protic and aprotic solvents (methanol, ethanol, acetone, DCM, DMF), oils, silicone oil, and saline solutions for at least 24 h exposure. Dielectric strength is typically ≥20 kV/mm. The material is melt‑processable with a suitable MFI for extrusion or injection molding; no curing step is required, so processing time remains well below the 30‑minute limit at 120 °C. Importantly, PEBA contains only polyether and amide monomers—no fluorinated or halogenated units—ensuring compliance with the PFAS‑free and environmental persistence constraints.

process_recipe.description: Prepare the processing equipment and set up the melt‑processing line.
process_recipe.conditions: Clean hopper, extruder barrel or injection molding barrel; ensure temperature control system is calibrated. Set target melt temperature to 120–140 °C (typical for PEBA 3000).

process_recipe.description: Feed the polymer into the hopper and allow it to melt.
process_recipe.conditions: Maintain barrel temperature at 120–140 °C; use a screw speed of 50–100 rpm (extruder) or injection pressure of 10–20 MPa (injection molding).

process_recipe.description: Melt‑process the polymer into the desired shape.
process_recipe.conditions: For extrusion: extrude filament at 120–140 °C, 10–20 MPa screw pressure; for injection molding: inject melt into mold cavity at 120–140 °C and 10–20 MPa. Hold melt for <30 min to ensure complete flow.

process_recipe.description: Cool the molded part or extruded filament in a water bath at ~25 °C to solidify and relieve residual stresses.
process_recipe.conditions: Water temperature 20–30 °C; cooling time 5–10 min depending on part thickness.

process_recipe.description: Demold and perform any post‑processing (e.g., trimming, surface finishing).
process_recipe.conditions: Standard lab handling; no additional curing or chemical treatment required.
